ops.body:
2026-07-21 9B CHECKPOINT RECOVERED (phase-4 weights decision: user chose recover-over-retrain; the fresh-retrain cost had corrected to ~$27 vs the $10-15 pre-approval). The 2026-07-18 qwen3.5-9b sweep run (Tinker-frame AUC 0.9371) is Tinker run 0b28a302-baa0-5029-ba11-e5c82fe9a06f — the account's only Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B run; identity corroborated by rank-32 match, sibling 8B/4B sweep runs sharing the identical 622-step schedule, last_request timestamp matching score_report.json mtime, and checkpoint steps at exactly the recipe's checkpoint_fracs.

FINAL checkpoint (PERMANENT, expires_at=None, HEAD-verified fetchable ~346MB):
tinker://0b28a302-baa0-5029-ba11-e5c82fe9a06f:train:0/sampler_weights/watcher-sft-622

CAVEAT: 0.9371 was the argmax over the run's 4 checkpoints (watcher-step00156/00311/00467 + sft-622); the per-checkpoint selection lived in the sweep's deleted staging journal. The 3 INTERMEDIATES EXPIRE 2026-07-25 ~09:15 UTC (7-day TTL). Promote step resolves this before serving: read server-side saved per-checkpoint val scores via the checkpoint_auc machinery (likely free); if absent, re-score the 4 against the deterministic frozen val split (seed 1729, small Tinker spend) BEFORE 07-25. Only then stage the winner.

Why local records had nothing (confirmed): the sweep is a pure observer — observe_fit_score journals into a throwaway staging dir that sweep.py rmtree's in a finally; only the scalar + base ids survive. Ops notes: Tinker key lives at ~/.cc-steer/tinker.env (NOT ~/.athome/tinker.env); a working py3.13 tinker venv is ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E37_moe/.venv (tinker 0.23.1, pyqwest 0.6.2 — avoids both the 3.14t athome venv and the pyqwest 0.7.0 TLS regression); verify scripts at /tmp/tinker_recover/.
